insight: P2 high-econ Vivid Drone opening: 1EE on turn 1, 1DD on turn 2, DDDD on turn 3 gives 12 gold turn 3 instead of 11 with DD DDEE, and enables flexible purchases (DDDD or DD1C). This line sacrifices option value for marginally higher efficiency if high econ is clearly optimal.
author: p0lari, mtanzer
context: Discussion of optimal early-game economy lines with Vivid Drone, comparing high-econ vs. flexible openings.

insight: P1 high-econ Vivid Drone opening: 1EE 11 DDDD (buying all remaining drones as vivids) is marginally more efficient than DD DDE DDD if committing to very high economy, but loses option value and future vivid purchase flexibility.
author: p0lari, mtanzer
context: Comparison of P1 opening lines with Vivid Drone emphasis versus drone-heavy economy.

insight: Vivid Drone economy: aim to maximize vivid drones in your economy (more efficient than regular drones) while keeping enough regular drones for tech clicks (Plexo Cell, Lucy) or emergency defense.
author: p0lari
context: General strategic advice on Vivid Drone composition in the economic tier.

insight: Xaetron soft-counters Valkyrie: Xaetron enables early defense that defuses Valkyrie's early abuse patterns (click Valk → hold Valk → buy Valk). In a Xaetron + Cynestra vs. Valkyrie + Cryo Ray matchup, the Xaetron side often wins despite Valkyrie being a solid standalone attacker.
author: apooche, mrguy888, crash_overlord
context: Testing a set with Xaetron, Cynestra, Valkyrie, and Cryo Ray; discussion of which unit combination is favored.

insight: Valkyrie without combos (Cryo Ray, absorb setups) is roughly Borehole Patroller tier in threat value; it becomes significantly stronger when paired with synergistic units or favorable defense configurations.
author: apooche
context: Evaluating Valkyrie's baseline power level without synergies in a mixed-unit set.

insight: Tatsu Nullifier + Xaetron as a P2 combo is extremely favored and difficult to counter; most responses either lose to the combo directly or to Xaetron + single tech (Animus/Blastforge). P1 Tatsu rushes without Xaetron can be viable if the set offers strong red defense (Auric Impulse, no cheap red attackers).
author: crash_overlord, mrguy888
context: Extended analysis of Tatsu + Xaetron strength and counter-strategies across different unit sets.

insight: Elyot Animus (Turn 2 Animus with 8 drones) is a legitimate and winning opening in rush-heavy sets, not an automatic loss for P2. Protoplasm + Symbiote is a strong defensive response, as is building Animus yourself on Turn 2.
author: iminabearsuit, wiwiweb, medar
context: Analysis of a game where P1 achieved Turn 2 Animus; discussion of whether it's a balanced or dominant strategy.

insight: Against pure red early rushers (e.g., Elyot Animus builds), DDC → DAE (Conduit → Animus + Engineers) can be superior to green-blue defenses, as it threatens Protoplasm while maintaining tempo. Getting first Protoplasm before opponent is a key defensive milestone.
author: medar, supergreyman
context: Strategic discussion on the correct P2 response to early red pressure and tech prioritization.

insight: Arms Race is a fundamentally weak card; it becomes even worse in Galvani Drone sets (Galvani provides defensive economy that competes with Arms Race value) or Electrovore sets, making it not worth considering in those contexts.
author: awaclus
context: Discussion of Arms Race viability in specific unit sets.

insight: Grimbotch spam (3-4 units): Using 3 Grimbotches is effective for setting up strong follow-up attackers (e.g., Bloodrager combos). The 4th Grimbotch does not generate real additional damage and may not die for full value; skipping it in favor of other tech or defense is often correct.
author: crash_overlord
context: Analysis of Grimbotch pressure as part of an aggro strategy; discussion of how many to purchase.

insight: Pixie value calculation: Lancetooth for 2 Pixies (8BBB total cost) is poor value compared to equivalent attack investment like Tarsiers (8RR for 2 units); Pixies should be bought only if they absorb enemy attackers that would have dealt real damage anyway, not as a primary attack vector.
author: steel0229e, p0lari
context: Analysis of a game involving Pixie and Lancetooth purchases; discussion of value efficiency.

insight: Against Pixie-heavy attacks, buying Engineers preemptively for granularity is better than buying Walls reactively, as Walls are inefficient blockers against small distributed damage. Husks can be more efficient than Walls in specific pixie matchups.
author: steel0229e
context: Game analysis identifying defensive inefficiency when blocking pixies with walls.

insight: Urban Sentry soft-counters Feral Warden pop strategies and can be a key defensive unit in Feral Warden sets. Standard P1 Feral Warden lines include DD DD DDA TTC (Drone/Drone, Drone/Drone, Drone/Animus, Tech/Tech/Conduit or similar).
author: liadahlia, p0lari
context: Set analysis including Feral Warden and Tortle; discussion of defensive and offensive tech choices.

insight: Sentry soft-counters both Frostbite and Hellhound; it is a high-value unit in sets featuring Feral Warden + Frostbite strategies, making it the recommended defensive approach.
author: liadahlia
context: Brief strategic note on Sentry as a defensive tool in Feral Warden sets.

insight: Venge Cannon + Thorium Dynamo combination can work as a punishment against low-economy breach-vulnerable rushes, or as a primary strategy in pure Venge sets. The combination is viable even with fast time controls (bullet games: 6 seconds per turn).
author: awaclus, steel0229e
context: Discussion of niche Venge + Dynamo synergy and when it applies.

insight: Gauss Charge and Pixie mechanic: Both disappear at end of turn and are not differentiated in damage/value equations despite Gauss Charge requiring Blue tech and Pixie requiring Green tech, because the resource type is context-dependent and both serve similar throughput roles.
author: apooche
context: Technical clarification on unit mechanic comparison during strategy discussion.

insight: Shock: a unit property describing units that threaten damage exceeding their tech cost. Drake threatens 4 damage with 2 tech (positive shock); Valkyrie threatens 4 damage with 4 tech (no shock).
author: mrguy888
context: Definition of community term during unit comparison discussion.

insight: In sets with multiple Cynestra supply, buying 3-4 Cynestras is difficult due to green color requirements for Cryo Ray support; typically only 1-2 Cynestras can be built with full Cryo support. Additional Cynestras become viable when Xaetron slows the game, allowing tech transition.
author: xyotsuba, mrguy888
context: Economic analysis of Cynestra scaling and color constraints.
